label: 2023 Ronde van Vlaanderen - Tour des Flandres
race_name: Ronde van Vlaanderen - Tour des Flandres

extraction.one_line_summary: POGACAR Tadej attacked repeatedly on the paired Oude Kwaremont-Paterberg climbs to drop VAN DER POEL Mathieu, winning solo by 15+ seconds.
tactical_patterns: POGACAR attacked relentlessly on paired Kwaremont-Paterberg to avoid sprint finish, proving Monument tactics prioritize format denial over energy conservation.
VAN DER POEL's mechanical chain drop on key climb proved decisive when chasing fractured breakaway quality riders.
Trek's PEDERSEN animated 100km+ breakaway forcing favorites to bridge rather than control, fragmenting Alpecin-Deceuninck domestique coverage.
VAN HOOYDONCK sacrificed own result to pace WOUT VAN AERT after weakness shown, revealing Jumbo-Visma prioritized podium salvage.

rider_signals.POGACAR Tadej: positive: Demonstrated Monument-winning tactical maturity by attacking repeatedly to guarantee solo finish rather than risk his inferior sprint.
rider_signals.VAN DER POEL Mathieu: negative: Mechanical at worst moment plus inability to match POGACAR's repeated accelerations exposes vulnerability when domestiques depleted by crashes.
rider_signals.WOUT VAN AERT Bernards: negative: Struggled visibly on climbs despite Jumbo-Visma numerical superiority, suggesting form insufficient for hilly Monument victory.
rider_signals.PEDERSEN Mads: positive: Fourth place from 100km+ breakaway shows capability to threaten favorites when race fractures early in windy Flemish classics.
rider_signals.POWLESS Neilson: positive: Fifth place finish demonstrates American climber's underrated cobbled classics capability when race becomes attritional.
